title: Albufeira et la grotte très connue de Benagil
creator: PG Drone (@pgdrone)
notes: CANDIDATE-SOURCED, INDEPENDENTLY RE-VERIFIED THIS SESSION -- one exemplar added to the sea-cave-and-natural-rock-arch coastal-landscape subgenre bucket, geographically distinct (Algarve, Portugal / Atlantic) from this pack's other sea-cave candidates. A prior agent's candidate JSON proposed this URL (https://www.youtube.com/shorts/T8JAyNIGxic); per this task's step 1 requirement it was NOT trusted verbatim -- it was re-verified live in this session via the YouTube oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/shorts/T8JAyNIGxic&format=json), which returned title="Albufeira et la grotte très connue de Benagil" (an exact match to the candidate's claimed title, modulo the missing accent in the candidate's plain-ASCII transcription), author_name="PG Drone", author_url="https://www.youtube.com/@pgdrone", and width=113/height=200 (a portrait aspect ratio confirming genuine /shorts/ vertical framing, not merely a URL-path convention) -- a field-for-field match to the candidate's title/creator/format claims, so this session's own primary-source check corroborates rather than merely repeats the prior agent's claim. This session additionally ran a WebSearch for "PG Drone" pgdrone youtube Benagil Albufeira drone pilot, which surfaced multiple other Benagil-cave/Algarve drone videos from unrelated creators/channels but found no independent corroborating coverage, follower-count figure, brand-client roster, or professional-standing signal for this specific channel beyond the oEmbed-confirmed channel name itself -- treated here as consistent with an individual/hobbyist creator channel rather than a verified professional operation (contrast with e.g. this pack's matthiasdangl-maldives-overwater-lagoon-fpv.json record, whose creator's brand-client roster was independently corroborated). `license_category` is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved': no Creative Commons marking could be located on the page (the canonical watch/shorts page returned a captcha/bot-check wall to this session's fetch attempts rather than renderable page content, so no licenseType/CC string could be searched for at all -- this is a harder verification failure than 'checked and found nothing', and is called out honestly here rather than conflated with a clean negative check). Per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise), `local_media_path` stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo, consistent with the spec's metadata-first default and its explicit prohibition on bulk-downloading all-rights-reserved footage. `duration` is left null: YouTube's oEmbed response does not include a duration field, and the captcha-blocked page fetch prevented reading the real <video> element's duration property in this session -- rather than fabricate a plausible-looking runtime, this field is left unset. `award_or_showcase_provenance` is left null: this is an individual creator's own-channel Shorts upload with no juried contest, festival, or broadcaster placement located for this specific video. `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the no-bulk-download constraint, and no live-playback review was achieved (see editorial_style below) to inform these estimates with direct visual observation. Calibration reasoning, consistent with this pack's existing ~0.71-0.85 range for individual/personal-channel (non-broadcaster, non-contest-verified) exemplars: sharpness=0.74 -- placed at this pack's individual-creator mid-tier, matching abovehorizon-lyngen-alps-fjord.json (0.74) and instagram-elisacappelletti-monument-valley.json (0.74), below the professionally-corroborated matthiasdangl (0.79) and contest-verified dronestagram-feature (0.85) tiers, since no camera platform, resolution, or bitrate claim could be verified for this specific upload and no professional-standing signal was found for this creator. exposure=0.73 -- a coastal daytime scene (sea cave/rock arch, Atlantic coastline, Algarve sun) is a generally favorable, evenly-lit condition, but without any confirmed exposure/dynamic-range signal for this exact clip this sits at this pack's middle band rather than its top, consistent with fishaholic-tt-tobago-lagoon-coastal.json's 0.75 tropical-lagoon coastal comparator but scored a touch lower given the total absence of any playback-derived signal here (that record at least achieved a prior live-playback duration/frame-sampling pass; this one achieved none). motion_smoothness=0.76 -- a 'fast aerial reveal' of a dramatic, well-known landmark subject (per the candidate's own framing) implies deliberate, confident piloting toward a specific reveal beat rather than aimless hovering, which supports a middle-of-pack rather than floor-tier score, but with no continuity/single-take confirmation possible (unlike matthiasdangl's confirmed clean full-timeline seek result) this stays below this pack's confirmed-single-take/professional tier (0.82-0.85) and in line with dereksturman-west-desert-utah-sand-dunes.json (0.74) and lido-wave-nazare-coastal.json (0.78), landing between the two. `composite_score` IS a real computation, not hand-computed: produced this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.74, exposure=0.73, motion_smoothness=0.76, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights()` via `.venv/bin/python3 -c "..."` in this session; `default_weights()` resolved to weights_version='default-v2' (0.25 each across sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness/composition). Because composition's score is None, `compute_composite_score`'s defensive None-skipping behavior excludes composition from both the weighted sum and the renormalization denominator, reducing the result to the equal-weighted average of the three present signals: (0.74+0.73+0.76)/3 = 0.7433333333333333, confirmed by actually running the function this session (not hand-computed), rounded to 0.7433 here. `editorial_style` reflects an honestly-hedged non-review: this task's own editorial-review data reports that no genuine editorial review was achieved. Browser-automation attempts were blocked by profile-lock/connection issues -- claude-in-chrome's extension was not connected, and Playwright MCP's Chrome profile (mcp-chrome-d5be8c7) was already held by another live Chrome process (verified via ps, not a stale lock), so navigation and even browser_close failed. Per the task's attempt budget, that review stopped retrying rather than force-killing a process that might belong to a concurrent session, rather than rabbit-holing. It then fell back to WebFetch on the source_url, which returned only truncated/empty content -- consistent with this session's own captcha-blocked fetch result noted above -- and a WebSearch, which surfaced only generic Benagil-cave tourism/travel results (boat tours, cave geology) with no description of this specific video's own cuts, shot lengths, or transitions. No usable basis for an editorial-style assessment was found by either that review pass or this authoring session's own independent checks, so `format`/`estimated_cut_count`/`avg_shot_length_seconds` stay null and `transition_styles_observed` stays empty rather than guessed; `review_method` is honestly set to 'not_reviewed' per this schema's honesty contract, since no live playback or even a renderable text description of this specific video's editing was ever obtained.
